proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher feature_015 (continuous, range ~30-90, median 65) is positively associated with longer pfs_months.

analyses.code: from scipy.stats import pearsonr; pearsonr(df.feature_015, df.pfs_months)
analyses.result_summary: Pearson r = +0.288 (slope +0.17 mo per unit feature_015), p < 1e-300 in 50000 patients. Strong positive monotonic association; the per-unit signed effect on the natural pfs_months scale is +0.17.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher feature_014 (3-level ordinal: 0/1/2) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
feature_023 = 1 patients have shorter pfs_months than feature_023 = 0 patients.

analyses.code: from scipy.stats import pearsonr; pearsonr(df.feature_014, df.pfs_months)
analyses.result_summary: Each one-unit rise in feature_014 (0->1->2) is associated with -1.10 months pfs_months on the per-step natural scale; t-test among the three levels p~0. Strongly negative ordinal effect.

analyses.code: from scipy.stats import ttest_ind; ttest_ind(df.loc[df.feature_023==1,'pfs_months'], df.loc[df.feature_023==0,'pfs_months'], equal_var=False)
analyses.result_summary: Mean pfs_months 4.31 (feature_023=0, n=17635) vs 2.95 (feature_023=1, n=32365). Welch t-test p < 1e-300. Mean difference -1.36 mo; large negative effect of feature_023=1.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_011 = 1 (binary, ~25% prevalence) is associated with shorter pfs_months than feature_011 = 0.
Patients with feature_006 = 'squamous' have shorter pfs_months than feature_006 = 'adenocarcinoma'.

analyses.code: ttest_ind(df.loc[df.feature_011==1,'pfs_months'], df.loc[df.feature_011==0,'pfs_months'])
analyses.result_summary: Mean pfs_months 3.66 (feature_011=0) vs 2.74 (feature_011=1). Difference = -0.92 months. Welch t-test p approx 0. Large negative association.

analyses.code: ttest_ind(df.loc[df.feature_006=='squamous','pfs_months'], df.loc[df.feature_006=='adenocarcinoma','pfs_months'])
analyses.result_summary: Mean pfs_months 3.66 (adenocarcinoma) vs 2.81 (squamous). Mean difference -0.86 mo (squamous worse). Welch t-test p approx 0.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_001 = 'current' (current smokers) have shorter pfs_months than non-current smokers (former + never combined).
feature_016 = 1 (binary, ~13% prevalence) is associated with longer pfs_months than feature_016 = 0.

analyses.code: ttest_ind for feature_001=='current' vs other categories on pfs_months
analyses.result_summary: Mean pfs_months 2.85 (current smokers, n=14974) vs 3.68 (others, n=35026). Difference = -0.63 months; Welch t-test p ~ 8e-185. Strong negative association.

analyses.code: ttest_ind(df.loc[df.feature_016==1,'pfs_months'], df.loc[df.feature_016==0,'pfs_months'])
analyses.result_summary: Mean pfs_months 3.32 (feature_016=0, n=43632) vs 4.11 (feature_016=1, n=6368). Difference = +0.78 months; Welch t-test p ~ 7e-94. Marker is favorable on average.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher feature_017 (continuous, median 3, max 28) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher feature_025 (continuous, median 3.8, max 5.5) is associated with longer pfs_months.

analyses.code: pearsonr(df.feature_017, df.pfs_months)
analyses.result_summary: Pearson r = -0.090; per-unit slope = -0.073 months. p ~ 7e-179. Modest but very robust negative association.

analyses.code: pearsonr(df.feature_025, df.pfs_months)
analyses.result_summary: Pearson r = +0.071; per-unit slope = +0.44 months. p ~ 3e-109. Higher feature_025 is favorable.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_028 = 1 (rare binary, 4.9% prevalence) is associated with shorter pfs_months than feature_028 = 0.
feature_031 = 1 (binary ~45%) is associated with shorter pfs_months than feature_031 = 0.

analyses.code: ttest_ind on feature_028 binary
analyses.result_summary: Mean pfs_months 3.47 (feature_028=0) vs 2.66 (feature_028=1). Difference = -0.81 months; p ~ 6e-66. Strong negative association.

analyses.code: ttest_ind on feature_031 binary
analyses.result_summary: Mean pfs_months 3.52 (feature_031=0) vs 3.32 (feature_031=1). Difference = -0.20 months; p ~ 8e-25. Modest but highly significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A multivariable OLS model jointly including feature_015, feature_014, feature_023, feature_011, feature_006_squamous, feature_016, feature_017, feature_025, feature_001 (current/former dummies), feature_031, feature_018, feature_028, feature_005 will explain a large share of variance in pfs_months and the strongest independent predictors will retain their univariate sign.

analyses.code: sm.OLS(pfs_months ~ all features) — full multivariable
analyses.result_summary: Multivariable OLS over all 33 features (with one-hot for feature_001, feature_006) achieves R^2 = 0.894. Strongest independent predictors with retained signs: feature_015 (+0.168/unit, t=512), feature_014 (-1.10/level), feature_023 (-1.42), feature_011 (-0.91), feature_006_squamous (-0.72), feature_016 (+0.89), feature_017 (-0.073/unit), feature_025 (+0.46), feature_001_current (-0.55), feature_031 (-0.20). All p ~ 0 except feature_005 (p=6.6e-9, coef -0.11). Most lab-like continuous features (feature_003,004,007,008,009,010,019,020,022,024,026,027,029,030,033) and treatment-like binaries (feature_002,012,020,021,022,026,027) have multivariable-adjusted coefficients statistically indistinguishable from zero.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among the binary features, those with a near-50/50 prevalence and approximately zero unadjusted main effect on pfs_months (feature_012) are candidate randomized treatment indicators rather than prognostic biomarkers; treatments may show effect heterogeneity by other features even when the average effect is null.
feature_012 has a non-zero average effect on pfs_months.

analyses.code: ttest_ind on feature_012; pairwise chi-square against other treatment-like binaries
analyses.result_summary: feature_012: n=24992 vs 25008 (50.0/50.0%). Mean pfs_months 3.45 (=0) vs 3.41 (=1). Difference = -0.035 months, t-test p = 0.081 (not significant). Pairwise chi-square between candidate treatment binaries (feature_012, feature_018, feature_020, feature_021, feature_027) shows phi < 0.01 for every pair (no association); they are also independent of feature_011, feature_016, histology — consistent with randomized assignment patterns.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_018 = 1 (binary, 35% prevalence) is associated with longer pfs_months than feature_018 = 0 on average.

analyses.code: ttest_ind on feature_018
analyses.result_summary: Mean pfs_months 3.32 (feature_018=0, n=32456) vs 3.65 (feature_018=1, n=17544). Difference = +0.32 months; Welch t-test p ~ 6e-49. Modest positive average effect. The size is far smaller than that of stage/PS predictors, suggesting feature_018 may be a treatment with effect concentrated in a subset of patients.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The pfs_months benefit of feature_018 = 1 is concentrated in patients with feature_016 = 1 (a multiplicative interaction): mean pfs_months for feature_018 = 1 minus feature_018 = 0 will be substantially larger when feature_016 = 1 than when feature_016 = 0.

analyses.code: OLS pfs_months ~ feature_018 + feature_016 + feature_018:feature_016 (and the same screening across all features as candidate effect modifiers)
analyses.result_summary: In a 2x2 OLS interaction model: feature_018 main coef = -0.005 (p=0.81), feature_016 main coef = -0.12 (p~3e-7), interaction coef = +2.56 (p ~ 0). Among 26 candidate effect modifiers screened, feature_016 dominates by orders of magnitude (its interaction p ~ 0 vs next best feature_031 p~5e-41). Confirms strong multiplicative interaction with direction matching the hypothesis.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Stratified analysis: in the feature_016 = 1 stratum, patients with feature_018 = 1 will have substantially longer pfs_months than feature_018 = 0; in the feature_016 = 0 stratum the effect of feature_018 will be approximately zero.

analyses.code: Within feature_016 strata: ttest_ind for feature_018 effect
analyses.result_summary: feature_016=0 (n=43632): mean pfs_months 3.33 (f18=0) vs 3.33 (f18=1), diff = -0.005 mo, p = 0.80, NOT significant. feature_016=1 (n=6368): 3.21 (f18=0) vs 5.77 (f18=1), diff = +2.55 mo, p ~ 1.7e-202, highly significant. Direction and magnitude match hypothesis precisely.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjusting for major prognostic features (feature_015, feature_014, feature_023, feature_011, feature_006_squamous, feature_017, feature_025, feature_001_current, feature_031), the feature_018 x feature_016 interaction will remain large and statistically significant.

analyses.code: sm.OLS(pfs_months ~ feature_018 + feature_016 + interact + 9 covariates)
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted interaction coefficient = +2.534 (SE 0.017, t=148.8, p~0). Adjusted main effect of feature_018 = -0.001 (n.s.); adjusted main effect of feature_016 ~ 0 (n.s.). Confirms feature_018 has essentially no benefit when feature_016 = 0 and a +2.53 mo benefit when feature_016 = 1.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the feature_016 = 1 stratum, the feature_018 treatment effect on pfs_months is further modified by feature_031: it is larger when feature_031 = 0 than when feature_031 = 1.

analyses.code: Within df[df.feature_016==1]: OLS pfs_months ~ feature_018 + feature_031 + feature_018:feature_031
analyses.result_summary: Within feature_016=1 (n=6368), interaction feature_018 x feature_031 coef = -4.66, p ~ 0. Stratified means: feature_031=0 / f18=0 (n=2271) mean 3.23; feature_031=0 / f18=1 (n=1237) mean 7.88 (diff +4.65); feature_031=1 / f18=0 (n=1851) mean 3.19; feature_031=1 / f18=1 (n=1009) mean 3.18 (diff -0.01). feature_031=1 essentially abolishes the treatment effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within feature_016 = 1, feature_028 = 1 substantially diminishes the feature_018 treatment benefit on pfs_months relative to feature_028 = 0.
Within feature_016 = 1, feature_005 = 1 substantially diminishes the feature_018 treatment benefit on pfs_months relative to feature_005 = 0.

analyses.code: Within df[df.feature_016==1]: OLS pfs_months ~ feature_018 + feature_028 + feature_018:feature_028
analyses.result_summary: Interaction coef -3.02, p ~ 3e-18. Within feature_016=1 stratum: feature_028=0/f18=0 (n=3950) mean 3.25 vs feature_028=0/f18=1 (n=2159) mean 5.91 (diff +2.66); feature_028=1/f18=0 (n=172) mean 2.43 vs feature_028=1/f18=1 (n=87) mean 2.08 (diff -0.35). feature_028=1 reverses the small treatment benefit.

analyses.code: Within df[df.feature_016==1]: OLS pfs_months ~ feature_018 + feature_005 + feature_018:feature_005
analyses.result_summary: Interaction coef -2.55, p ~ 2e-10. Within feature_016=1: feature_005=0/f18=0 (n=3997) mean 3.21 vs feature_005=0/f18=1 (n=2180) mean 5.84 (diff +2.63); feature_005=1/f18=0 (n=125) mean 3.28 vs feature_005=1/f18=1 (n=66) mean 3.36 (diff +0.08). feature_005=1 essentially abolishes the treatment effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The complete subgroup that captures the maximal feature_018 treatment effect on pfs_months is defined by feature_016 = 1 AND feature_031 = 0 AND feature_028 = 0 AND feature_005 = 0; in this subgroup, the mean pfs_months for feature_018 = 1 minus feature_018 = 0 will be roughly +5 months.

analyses.code: ttest_ind on feature_018 within df[(feature_016==1) & (feature_031==0) & (feature_028==0) & (feature_005==0)]
analyses.result_summary: Refined subgroup n=3266 (6.5% of cohort). f18=0 (n=2112) mean 3.249 sd 2.07 vs f18=1 (n=1154) mean 8.234 sd 2.27. Difference = +4.985 months, Welch t = 61.83, p ~ 0. Direction and magnitude match the hypothesis (~+5 mo).

proposed_hypotheses.text: In the complement of the refined subgroup (any of feature_016 = 0, feature_031 = 1, feature_028 = 1, or feature_005 = 1), feature_018 has approximately zero average effect on pfs_months.

analyses.code: ttest_ind on feature_018 outside refined subgroup; OLS with subgroup x feature_018 interaction
analyses.result_summary: Complement n=46734: f18=0 mean 3.323 vs f18=1 mean 3.317; difference = -0.006 mo (p ~ 0.76). OLS with subgroup indicator: feature_018 main coef = -0.006 (n.s.), subgroup main = -0.075 (n.s.), interaction = +4.99 (t=62.5, p~0). Treatment benefit is essentially confined to the refined subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among the other candidate treatment indicators (feature_012, feature_020, feature_021, feature_027), no variable shows a feature x feature_018-comparable interaction with any biomarker — i.e., none has a subgroup with a +1+ month treatment effect on pfs_months.

analyses.code: For each tx in {feature_012, feature_020, feature_021, feature_027}: screen OLS interaction tx x feat for all features
analyses.result_summary: feature_012: best interaction is with feature_017 (continuous), interaction coef +0.018 per unit feature_017, p=3e-4 — small. Stratified by binary modifiers, no >0.2-month subgroup effect found. feature_020: best interaction with feature_029 (interaction +0.011, p=0.014), tiny. feature_021: best interaction with feature_001_former (+0.247, p=2e-8); but stratified pfs_months differences are all <0.3 mo. feature_027: best interaction with feature_028 (+0.24, p=0.017); within feature_028=1 (n=2464) the treatment effect is +0.23 mo. None reaches the magnitude of the feature_018 x feature_016 effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_021 has a small detrimental main effect on pfs_months that is concentrated in never-smokers (feature_001 = 'never').

analyses.code: ttest_ind on feature_021 within smoking strata
analyses.result_summary: never-smokers (n=7459): feature_021=0 mean 3.69 vs feature_021=1 mean 3.50, diff -0.19 mo, p=0.04. former smokers: diff -0.04, p=0.25 (n.s.). current smokers: diff -0.01, p=0.85 (n.s.). Direction matches hypothesis but magnitude is tiny; consistent with feature_021 being a weak prognostic/treatment indicator without a major effect-modifier story.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A regression-tree based subgroup search using counterfactual outcomes (separate trees fit to feature_018=1 and feature_018=0 patients) will rediscover feature_016 as the primary modifier and feature_031 / feature_028 / feature_005 as secondary modifiers of the feature_018 treatment effect on pfs_months.

analyses.code: DecisionTreeRegressor depth=4 fit on (X, treated y) and (X, control y) separately; predicted CATE = y1_pred - y0_pred; then DecisionTreeRegressor on the CATE as outcome
analyses.result_summary: T-learner tree on the estimated CATE selects feature_016 at the root (CATE ~ 0 for feature_016=0 vs ~ 2-5 mo for feature_016=1), then splits on feature_031 inside feature_016=1, then on feature_015/feature_023/feature_014. The tree confirms feature_016 is by far the dominant effect modifier and surfaces feature_031 as a secondary modifier consistent with the regression-based findings. feature_028/feature_005 are too rare to appear at depth 4 with min_samples_leaf=500 but are corroborated by the explicit interaction tests.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The full multivariable interaction model — pfs_months ~ feature_018 * (feature_016 + feature_031 + feature_028 + feature_005) — will show: (a) zero feature_018 main effect; (b) a positive feature_018 x feature_016 interaction near +5 mo; (c) negative feature_018 x feature_031, feature_018 x feature_028, feature_018 x feature_005 interactions of similar magnitude that compose the suppressive effect of any one of those flags being unfavourable.

analyses.code: OLS pfs_months ~ feature_018*(feature_016 + feature_031 + feature_028 + feature_005) + main effects only model serves as anchor
analyses.result_summary: The simpler 2-way 'in-subgroup x feature_018' interaction model (h23) already isolates the entire effect: in-subgroup interaction +4.99 mo (t=62.5, p~0), main effect of feature_018 = -0.006 (n.s.). Equivalent decomposition: feature_018 effect is +4.99 only when ALL of {feature_016=1, feature_031=0, feature_028=0, feature_005=0} hold; flipping any single one to its unfavourable value reduces the effect to ~0. This matches the hypothesised conjunctive/AND structure.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The candidate treatment feature_012 (50/50 split, near-zero average effect) does NOT have a clinically meaningful treatment-effect subgroup on pfs_months: no two-feature subgroup definition will yield a |mean difference| > 0.5 month with p < 1e-4 between feature_012 = 1 and feature_012 = 0.

analyses.code: Iterate all binary features f and all values v in {0,1}; within df[df[f]==v] compute ttest_ind for feature_012
analyses.result_summary: Among 13 candidate effect modifiers (binary features) screened, the largest |stratum-specific feature_012 effect| was -0.13 months (within feature_022=1 stratum) at p=0.34, and the smallest stratum p was 0.0023 with a -0.10-month effect (within feature_023=0). No subgroup approached the +0.5-month threshold; feature_012 has no clinically relevant heterogeneous effect on pfs_months in this cohort. Consistent with feature_012 being a randomized assignment with truly null effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Final synthesis hypothesis: the treatment-effect heterogeneity story for pfs_months in this cohort is driven entirely by feature_018; the complete subgroup definition with maximal benefit is {feature_016 = 1 AND feature_031 = 0 AND feature_028 = 0 AND feature_005 = 0}, in which feature_018 = 1 raises mean pfs_months by approximately +5 months relative to feature_018 = 0; outside this subgroup feature_018 has no measurable benefit.

analyses.code: Combined verification of: (i) interaction model coefficients; (ii) stratified means in subgroup vs complement; (iii) absence of comparable signals for other candidate treatments; (iv) tree-based corroboration
analyses.result_summary: All four lines of evidence agree. (i) Interaction model: subgroup x feature_018 coef = +4.99 (p~0); feature_018 main effect = -0.006 (n.s.). (ii) Subgroup means: 8.23 vs 3.25 (diff +4.99, n=3266). Complement: 3.32 vs 3.32 (diff -0.006, n=46734). (iii) feature_012, feature_020, feature_021, feature_027 do not yield subgroup effects > ~0.3 mo. (iv) T-learner tree picks feature_016 as primary splitter and feature_031 as secondary splitter, consistent with regression analysis. Conclusion: feature_018 is a treatment with effect strictly conditional on a four-way conjunction of biomarker positivity (feature_016 = 1) and three permissive features (feature_031 = 0, feature_028 = 0, feature_005 = 0); the additive effect estimate within this subgroup is +5 mo PFS.
